analysis.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission completed a historic lunar flyby on Monday, with the four-person crew breaking Apollo 13's record for the farthest distance any humans have traveled from Earth. The spacecraft passed behind the moon's far side, causing a planned approximately 40-minute communications blackout before contact was reestablished. The crew also observed a solar eclipse, named a lunar crater after commander Reid Wiseman's late wife Carroll, and received a congratulatory call from President Trump before beginning their return journey to Earth, with splashdown expected Friday.

analysis.summary: President Trump set a Tuesday deadline (8 p.m. ET) for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, threatening massive military strikes on civilian infrastructure including bridges and power plants if no deal is reached. Iran responded defiantly, conveying a 10-point cease-fire proposal through Pakistani intermediaries while mobilizing youth to form human chains around power plants. The crisis, occurring on approximately day 39 of U.S.-Israeli military operations against Iran, triggered market volatility, a UN vote on a related resolution, and regional security responses including the closure of the King Fahd Causeway.
